sn42.8:1.1.pli: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā nāḷandāyaṁ viharati pāvārikambavane.
sn42.8:1.1.eng: At one time the Buddha was staying near Nāḷandā in Pāvārika’s mango grove.

sn42.8:1.2.pli: Atha kho asibandhakaputto gāmaṇi nigaṇṭhasāvako yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho asibandhakaputtaṁ gāmaṇiṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
sn42.8:1.2.eng: Then Asibandhaka’s son the chief, who was a disciple of the Jains, went up to the Buddha, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him,

sn42.8:1.3.pli: “kathaṁ nu kho, gāmaṇi, nigaṇṭho nāṭaputto sāvakānaṁ dhammaṁ desetī”ti?
sn42.8:1.3.eng: “Chief, how does the Jain ascetic of the Ñātika clan teach his disciples?”

sn42.8:1.4.pli: “Evaṁ kho, bhante, nigaṇṭho nāṭaputto sāvakānaṁ dhammaṁ deseti:
sn42.8:1.4.eng: “Sir, this is how the Jain Ñātika teaches his disciples:

sn42.8:1.5.pli: ‘yo koci pāṇaṁ atipāteti, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayiko, yo koci adinnaṁ ādiyati, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayiko, yo koci kāmesu micchā carati, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayiko, yo koci musā bhaṇati sabbo, so āpāyiko nerayiko.
sn42.8:1.5.eng: ‘Everyone who kills a living creature, steals, commits sexual misconduct, or lies goes to a place of loss, to hell.

sn42.8:1.6.pli: Yaṁbahulaṁ yaṁbahulaṁ viharati, tena tena nīyatī’ti.
sn42.8:1.6.eng: You’re led on by what you usually live by.’

sn42.8:1.7.pli: Evaṁ kho, bhante, nigaṇṭho nāṭaputto sāvakānaṁ dhammaṁ desetī”ti.
sn42.8:1.7.eng: This is how the Jain Ñātika teaches his disciples.”

sn42.8:1.8.pli: “‘Yaṁbahulaṁ yaṁbahulañca, gāmaṇi, viharati, tena tena nīyati’, evaṁ sante na koci āpāyiko nerayiko bhavissati, yathā nigaṇṭhassa nāṭaputtassa vacanaṁ.
sn42.8:1.8.eng: “‘You’re led on by what you usually live by’: if this were true, then, according to what the Jain Ñātika says, no-one would go to a place of loss, to hell.

sn42.8:2.2.pli: yo so puriso pāṇātipātī rattiyā vā divasassa vā samayāsamayaṁ upādāya, katamo bahutaro samayo, yaṁ vā so pāṇamatipāteti, yaṁ vā so pāṇaṁ nātipātetī”ti?
sn42.8:2.2.eng: Take an individual who kills living creatures. If we compare periods of time during the day and night, which is more frequent: the occasions when they’re killing or when they’re not killing?”

sn42.8:2.3.pli: “Yo so, bhante, puriso pāṇātipātī rattiyā vā divasassa vā samayāsamayaṁ upādāya, appataro so samayo yaṁ so pāṇamatipāteti, atha kho sveva bahutaro samayo yaṁ so pāṇaṁ nātipātetī”ti.
sn42.8:2.3.eng: “The occasions when they’re killing are less frequent, while the occasions when they’re not killing are more frequent.”

sn42.8:2.4.pli: “‘Yaṁbahulaṁ yaṁbahulañca, gāmaṇi, viharati tena tena nīyatī’ti, evaṁ sante na koci āpāyiko nerayiko bhavissati, yathā nigaṇṭhassa nāṭaputtassa vacanaṁ.
sn42.8:2.4.eng: “‘You’re led on by what you usually live by’: if this were true, then, according to what the Jain ascetic of the Ñātika clan says, no-one would go to a place of loss, to hell.

sn42.8:3.2.pli: yo so puriso adinnādāyī rattiyā vā divasassa vā samayāsamayaṁ upādāya, katamo bahutaro samayo, yaṁ vā so adinnaṁ ādiyati, yaṁ vā so adinnaṁ nādiyatī”ti.

sn42.8:3.3.pli: “Yo so, bhante, puriso adinnādāyī rattiyā vā divasassa vā samayāsamayaṁ upādāya appataro so samayo, yaṁ so adinnaṁ ādiyati, atha kho sveva bahutaro samayo, yaṁ so adinnaṁ nādiyatī”ti.

sn42.8:3.4.pli: “‘Yaṁbahulaṁ yaṁbahulañca, gāmaṇi, viharati tena tena nīyatī’ti, evaṁ sante na koci āpāyiko nerayiko bhavissati, yathā nigaṇṭhassa nāṭaputtassa vacanaṁ.

sn42.8:4.1.pli: Taṁ kiṁ maññasi, gāmaṇi, yo so puriso kāmesumicchācārī rattiyā vā divasassa vā samayāsamayaṁ upādāya, katamo bahutaro samayo, yaṁ vā so kāmesu micchā carati, yaṁ vā so kāmesu micchā na caratī”ti?
sn42.8:4.1.eng: Take an individual who commits sexual misconduct …

sn42.8:4.2.pli: “Yo so, bhante, puriso kāmesumicchācārī rattiyā vā divasassa vā samayāsamayaṁ upādāya, appataro so samayo yaṁ so kāmesu micchā carati, atha kho sveva bahutaro samayo, yaṁ so kāmesu micchā na caratī”ti.

sn42.8:4.3.pli: “‘Yaṁbahulaṁ yaṁbahulañca, gāmaṇi, viharati tena tena nīyatī’ti, evaṁ sante na koci āpāyiko nerayiko bhavissati, yathā nigaṇṭhassa nāṭaputtassa vacanaṁ.

sn42.8:5.1.pli: Taṁ kiṁ maññasi, gāmaṇi, yo so puriso musāvādī rattiyā vā divasassa vā samayāsamayaṁ upādāya, katamo bahutaro samayo, yaṁ vā so musā bhaṇati, yaṁ vā so musā na bhaṇatī”ti?
sn42.8:5.1.eng: Take an individual who lies. If we compare periods of time during the day and night, which is more frequent: the occasions when they’re lying or when they’re not lying?”

sn42.8:5.2.pli: “Yo so, bhante, puriso musāvādī rattiyā vā divasassa vā samayāsamayaṁ upādāya, appataro so samayo, yaṁ so musā bhaṇati, atha kho sveva bahutaro samayo, yaṁ so musā na bhaṇatī”ti.
sn42.8:5.2.eng: “The occasions when they’re lying are less frequent, while the occasions when they’re not lying are more frequent.”

sn42.8:5.3.pli: “‘Yaṁbahulaṁ yaṁbahulañca, gāmaṇi, viharati tena tena nīyatī’ti, evaṁ sante na koci āpāyiko nerayiko bhavissati, yathā nigaṇṭhassa nāṭaputtassa vacanaṁ.
sn42.8:5.3.eng: “‘You’re led on by what you usually live by’: if this were true, then, according to what the Jain Ñātika says, no-one would go to a place of loss, to hell.

sn42.8:6.1.pli: Idha, gāmaṇi, ekacco satthā evaṁvādī hoti evaṁdiṭṭhi:
sn42.8:6.1.eng: Take some teacher who has this doctrine and view:

sn42.8:6.2.pli: ‘yo koci pāṇamatipāteti, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayiko, yo koci adinnaṁ ādiyati, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayiko, yo koci kāmesu micchā carati, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayiko, yo koci musā bhaṇati, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayiko’ti.
sn42.8:6.2.eng: ‘Everyone who kills a living creature, steals, commits sexual misconduct, or lies goes to a place of loss, to hell.’

sn42.8:6.3.pli: Tasmiṁ kho pana, gāmaṇi, satthari sāvako abhippasanno hoti.
sn42.8:6.3.eng: And there’s a disciple who is devoted to that teacher.

sn42.8:6.5.eng: ‘My teacher has this doctrine and view:

sn42.8:6.6.pli: yo koci pāṇamatipāteti, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayikoti.
sn42.8:6.6.eng: “Everyone who kills a living creature, steals, commits sexual misconduct, or lies goes to a place of loss, to hell.”

sn42.8:6.7.pli: Atthi kho pana mayā pāṇo atipātito, ahampamhi āpāyiko nerayikoti diṭṭhiṁ paṭilabhati.

sn42.8:6.8.pli: Taṁ, gāmaṇi, vācaṁ appahāya taṁ cittaṁ appahāya taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissajjitvā yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ niraye.

sn42.8:6.10.pli: yo koci adinnaṁ ādiyati, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayikoti.

sn42.8:6.11.pli: Atthi kho pana mayā adinnaṁ ādinnaṁ ahampamhi āpāyiko nerayikoti diṭṭhiṁ paṭilabhati.

sn42.8:6.12.pli: Taṁ, gāmaṇi, vācaṁ appahāya taṁ cittaṁ appahāya taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissajjitvā yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ niraye.

sn42.8:6.14.pli: yo koci kāmesu micchā carati, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayiko’ti.

sn42.8:6.15.pli: Atthi kho pana mayā kāmesu micchā ciṇṇaṁ.

sn42.8:6.17.pli: Taṁ, gāmaṇi, vācaṁ appahāya taṁ cittaṁ appahāya taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissajjitvā yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ niraye.

sn42.8:6.19.pli: yo koci musā bhaṇati, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayikoti.

sn42.8:6.20.pli: Atthi kho pana mayā musā bhaṇitaṁ.

sn42.8:6.21.eng: They get the view: ‘I too am going to a place of loss, to hell.’

sn42.8:6.22.pli: Taṁ, gāmaṇi, vācaṁ appahāya taṁ cittaṁ appahāya taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissajjitvā yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ niraye.
sn42.8:6.22.eng: Unless they give up that speech and that thought, and let go of that view, they will be cast down to hell.

sn42.8:7.1.pli: Idha pana, gāmaṇi, tathāgato loke uppajjati arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vijjācaraṇasampanno sugato lokavidū anuttaro purisadammasārathi satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā.
sn42.8:7.1.eng: But consider when a Realized One arises in the world, perfected, a fully awakened Buddha, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, holy, knower of the world, supreme guide for those fit for training, teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.

sn42.8:7.2.pli: So anekapariyāyena pāṇātipātaṁ garahati vigarahati, ‘pāṇātipātā viramathā’ti cāha.
sn42.8:7.2.eng: In many ways he criticizes and denounces killing living creatures, saying: ‘Stop killing living creatures!’

sn42.8:7.6.pli: Tasmiṁ kho pana, gāmaṇi, satthari sāvako abhippasanno hoti.
sn42.8:7.6.eng: And there’s a disciple who is devoted to that teacher.

sn42.8:7.8.pli: ‘bhagavā kho anekapariyāyena pāṇātipātaṁ garahati vigarahati, pāṇātipātā viramathāti cāha.
sn42.8:7.8.eng: ‘In many ways the Buddha criticizes and denounces killing living creatures, saying: “Stop killing living creatures!”

sn42.8:7.9.pli: Atthi kho pana mayā pāṇo atipātito yāvatako vā tāvatako vā.
sn42.8:7.9.eng: But I have killed living creatures to a certain extent.

sn42.8:7.10.pli: Yo kho pana mayā pāṇo atipātito yāvatako vā tāvatako vā, taṁ na suṭṭhu, taṁ na sādhu.
sn42.8:7.10.eng: That’s not right, it’s not good,

sn42.8:7.11.pli: Ahañceva kho pana tappaccayā vippaṭisārī assaṁ.
sn42.8:7.11.eng: and I feel remorseful because of it.

sn42.8:7.12.pli: Na metaṁ pāpaṁ kammaṁ akataṁ bhavissatī’ti.
sn42.8:7.12.eng: But I can’t undo what I have done.’

sn42.8:7.13.pli: So iti paṭisaṅkhāya tañceva pāṇātipātaṁ pajahati.
sn42.8:7.13.eng: Reflecting like this, they give up killing living creatures,

sn42.8:7.14.eng: and in future they don’t kill living creatures.

sn42.8:7.15.eng: That’s how to give up this bad deed

sn42.8:8.1.pli: ‘Bhagavā kho anekapariyāyena adinnādānaṁ garahati vigarahati, adinnādānā viramathāti cāha.
sn42.8:8.1.eng: ‘In many ways the Buddha criticizes and denounces stealing …’

sn42.8:8.2.pli: Atthi kho pana mayā adinnaṁ ādinnaṁ yāvatakaṁ vā tāvatakaṁ vā.

sn42.8:8.3.pli: Yaṁ kho pana mayā adinnaṁ ādinnaṁ yāvatakaṁ vā tāvatakaṁ vā taṁ na suṭṭhu, taṁ na sādhu.

sn42.8:8.4.pli: Ahañceva kho pana tappaccayā vippaṭisārī assaṁ, na metaṁ pāpaṁ kammaṁ akataṁ bhavissatī’ti.

sn42.8:8.5.pli: So iti paṭisaṅkhāya tañceva adinnādānaṁ pajahati.

sn42.8:9.1.pli: ‘Bhagavā kho pana anekapariyāyena kāmesumicchācāraṁ garahati vigarahati, kāmesumicchācārā viramathāti cāha.
sn42.8:9.1.eng: ‘In many ways the Buddha criticizes and denounces sexual misconduct …’

sn42.8:9.2.pli: Atthi kho pana mayā kāmesu micchā ciṇṇaṁ yāvatakaṁ vā tāvatakaṁ vā.

sn42.8:9.3.pli: Yaṁ kho pana mayā kāmesu micchā ciṇṇaṁ yāvatakaṁ vā tāvatakaṁ vā taṁ na suṭṭhu, taṁ na sādhu.

sn42.8:9.4.pli: Ahañceva kho pana tappaccayā vippaṭisārī assaṁ, na metaṁ pāpaṁ kammaṁ akataṁ bhavissatī’ti.

sn42.8:9.5.pli: So iti paṭisaṅkhāya tañceva kāmesumicchācāraṁ pajahati, āyatiñca kāmesumicchācārā paṭivirato hoti.

sn42.8:10.1.pli: ‘Bhagavā kho pana anekapariyāyena musāvādaṁ garahati vigarahati, musāvādā viramathāti cāha.
sn42.8:10.1.eng: ‘In many ways the Buddha criticizes and denounces lying, saying: “Stop lying!”

sn42.8:10.2.pli: Atthi kho pana mayā musā bhaṇitaṁ yāvatakaṁ vā tāvatakaṁ vā.
sn42.8:10.2.eng: But I have lied to a certain extent.

sn42.8:10.3.pli: Yaṁ kho pana mayā musā bhaṇitaṁ yāvatakaṁ vā tāvatakaṁ vā taṁ na suṭṭhu, taṁ na sādhu. Ahañceva kho pana tappaccayā vippaṭisārī assaṁ, na metaṁ pāpaṁ kammaṁ akataṁ bhavissatī’ti.
sn42.8:10.3.eng: That’s not right, it’s not good, and I feel remorseful because of it. But I can’t undo what I have done.’

sn42.8:10.4.pli: So iti paṭisaṅkhāya tañceva musāvādaṁ pajahati, āyatiñca musāvādā paṭivirato hoti.
sn42.8:10.4.eng: Reflecting like this, they give up lying, and in future they refrain from lying.

sn42.8:10.5.pli: Evametassa pāpassa kammassa pahānaṁ hoti. Evametassa pāpassa kammassa samatikkamo hoti.
sn42.8:10.5.eng: That’s how to give up this bad deed and get past it.

sn42.8:11.1.pli: So pāṇātipātaṁ pahāya pāṇātipātā paṭivirato hoti.
sn42.8:11.1.eng: They give up killing living creatures.

sn42.8:11.5.pli: Pisuṇaṁ vācaṁ pahāya pisuṇāya vācāya paṭivirato hoti.

sn42.8:11.6.pli: Pharusaṁ vācaṁ pahāya pharusāya vācāya paṭivirato hoti.

sn42.8:11.9.eng: They give up ill will and malevolence.

sn42.8:11.10.eng: They give up wrong view and have right view.

sn42.8:12.1.pli: Sa kho so, gāmaṇi, ariyasāvako evaṁ vigatābhijjho vigatabyāpādo asammūḷho sampajāno paṭissato mettāsahagatena cetasā ekaṁ disaṁ pharitvā viharati, tathā dutiyaṁ, tathā tatiyaṁ, tathā catutthaṁ. Iti uddhamadho tiriyaṁ sabbadhi sabbattatāya sabbāvantaṁ lokaṁ mettāsahagatena cetasā vipulena mahaggatena appamāṇena averena abyāpajjena pharitvā viharati.
sn42.8:12.1.eng: That noble disciple is rid of desire, rid of ill will, unconfused, aware, and mindful. They meditate spreading a heart full of love to one direction, and to the second, and to the third, and to the fourth. In the same way above, below, across, everywhere, all around, they spread a heart full of love to the whole world—abundant, expansive, limitless, free of enmity and ill will.

sn42.8:12.2.pli: Seyyathāpi, gāmaṇi, balavā saṅkhadhamo appakasireneva catuddisā viññāpeyya;
sn42.8:12.2.eng: Suppose there was a powerful horn blower. They’d easily make themselves heard in the four quarters.

sn42.8:12.3.pli: evameva kho, gāmaṇi, evaṁ bhāvitāya mettāya cetovimuttiyā evaṁ bahulīkatāya yaṁ pamāṇakataṁ kammaṁ, na taṁ tatrāvasissati, na taṁ tatrāvatiṭṭhati.
sn42.8:12.3.eng: In the same way, when the heart’s release by love has been developed and cultivated like this, any limited deeds they’ve done don’t remain or persist there.

sn42.8:13.1.pli: Sa kho so, gāmaṇi, ariyasāvako evaṁ vigatābhijjho vigatabyāpādo asammūḷho sampajāno paṭissato karuṇāsahagatena cetasā …pe…
sn42.8:13.1.eng: Then that noble disciple is rid of desire, rid of ill will, unconfused, aware, and mindful. They meditate spreading a heart full of compassion …

sn42.8:13.2.eng: They meditate spreading a heart full of rejoicing …

sn42.8:13.3.pli: Upekkhāsahagatena cetasā ekaṁ disaṁ pharitvā viharati, tathā dutiyaṁ, tathā tatiyaṁ, tathā catutthaṁ. Iti uddhamadho tiriyaṁ sabbadhi sabbattatāya sabbāvantaṁ lokaṁ upekkhāsahagatena cetasā vipulena mahaggatena appamāṇena averena abyāpajjena pharitvā viharati.
sn42.8:13.3.eng: They meditate spreading a heart full of equanimity to one direction, and to the second, and to the third, and to the fourth. In the same way above, below, across, everywhere, all around, they spread a heart full of equanimity to the whole world—abundant, expansive, limitless, free of enmity and ill will.

sn42.8:13.4.pli: Seyyathāpi, gāmaṇi, balavā saṅkhadhamo appakasireneva catuddisā viññāpeyya;
sn42.8:13.4.eng: Suppose there was a powerful horn blower. They’d easily make themselves heard in the four quarters.

sn42.8:13.5.pli: evameva kho, gāmaṇi, evaṁ bhāvitāya upekkhāya cetovimuttiyā evaṁ bahulīkatāya yaṁ pamāṇakataṁ kammaṁ na taṁ tatrāvasissati, na taṁ tatrāvatiṭṭhatī”ti.
sn42.8:13.5.eng: In the same way, when the heart’s release by equanimity has been developed and cultivated like this, any limited deeds they’ve done don’t remain or persist there.”

sn42.8:14.1.pli: Evaṁ vutte, asibandhakaputto gāmaṇi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn42.8:14.1.eng: When he said this, Asibandhaka’s son the chief said to the Buddha,

sn42.8:14.3.pli: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhagavā dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.
sn42.8:14.3.eng: From this day forth, may the Buddha remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

sn42.9:1.1.pli: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosalesu cārikaṁ caramāno mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ yena nāḷandā tadavasari.
sn42.9:1.1.eng: At one time the Buddha was wandering in the land of the Kosalans together with a large Saṅgha of mendicants when he arrived at Nāḷandā.

sn42.9:1.2.pli: Tatra sudaṁ bhagavā nāḷandāyaṁ viharati pāvārikambavane.
sn42.9:1.2.eng: There he stayed near Nāḷandā in Pāvārika’s mango grove.

sn42.9:2.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena nāḷandā dubbhikkhā hoti dvīhitikā setaṭṭhikā salākāvuttā.
sn42.9:2.1.eng: Now that was a time of famine and scarcity in Nāḷandā, with blighted crops turned to straw.

sn42.9:2.2.pli: Tena kho pana samayena nigaṇṭho nāṭaputto nāḷandāyaṁ paṭivasati mahatiyā nigaṇṭhaparisāya saddhiṁ.
sn42.9:2.2.eng: At that time the Jain ascetic of the Ñātika clan was residing at Nāḷandā together with a large assembly of Jain ascetics.

sn42.9:2.3.pli: Atha kho asibandhakaputto gāmaṇi nigaṇṭhasāvako yena nigaṇṭho nāṭaputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā nigaṇṭhaṁ nāṭaputtaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho asibandhakaputtaṁ gāmaṇiṁ nigaṇṭho nāṭaputto etadavoca:
sn42.9:2.3.eng: Then Asibandhaka’s son the chief, who was a disciple of the Jains, went up to the Jain Ñātika, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Jain Ñātika said to him:

sn42.9:2.4.pli: “ehi tvaṁ, gāmaṇi, samaṇassa gotamassa vādaṁ āropehi.
sn42.9:2.4.eng: “Come, chief, refute the ascetic Gotama’s doctrine.

sn42.9:2.5.eng: Then you will get a good reputation:

sn42.9:2.6.pli: ‘asibandhakaputtena gāmaṇinā samaṇassa gotamassa evaṁmahiddhikassa evaṁmahānubhāvassa vādo āropito’”ti.
sn42.9:2.6.eng: ‘Asibandhaka’s son the chief refuted the doctrine of the ascetic Gotama, so mighty and powerful!’”

sn42.9:3.1.pli: “Kathaṁ panāhaṁ, bhante, samaṇassa gotamassa evaṁmahiddhikassa evaṁmahānubhāvassa vādaṁ āropessāmī”ti?
sn42.9:3.1.eng: “But sir, how am I to do this?”

sn42.9:3.2.pli: “Ehi tvaṁ, gāmaṇi, yena samaṇo gotamo tenupasaṅkama; upasaṅkamitvā samaṇaṁ gotamaṁ evaṁ vadehi:
sn42.9:3.2.eng: “Here, brahmin, go to the ascetic Gotama and say to him:

sn42.9:3.3.pli: ‘nanu, bhante, bhagavā anekapariyāyena kulānaṁ anuddayaṁ vaṇṇeti, anurakkhaṁ vaṇṇeti, anukampaṁ vaṇṇetī’ti?
sn42.9:3.3.eng: ‘Sir, don’t you in many ways praise kindness, protection, and sympathy for families?’

sn42.9:3.4.pli: Sace kho, gāmaṇi, samaṇo gotamo evaṁ puṭṭho evaṁ byākaroti:
sn42.9:3.4.eng: When he’s asked this, if he answers:

sn42.9:3.5.pli: ‘evaṁ, gāmaṇi, tathāgato anekapariyāyena kulānaṁ anuddayaṁ vaṇṇeti, anurakkhaṁ vaṇṇeti, anukampaṁ vaṇṇetī’ti, tamenaṁ tvaṁ evaṁ vadeyyāsi:
sn42.9:3.5.eng: ‘Indeed I do, chief,’ say this to him:

sn42.9:3.6.pli: ‘atha kiñcarahi, bhante, bhagavā dubbhikkhe dvīhitike setaṭṭhike salākāvutte mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ cārikaṁ carati?
sn42.9:3.6.eng: ‘So what then are you doing, wandering together with this large Saṅgha of mendicants during a time of famine and scarcity, with blighted crops turned to straw?

sn42.9:3.7.pli: Ucchedāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno, anayāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno, upaghātāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno’ti.
sn42.9:3.7.eng: The Buddha is practicing to annihilate, collapse, and ruin families!’

sn42.9:3.8.pli: Imaṁ kho te, gāmaṇi, samaṇo gotamo ubhatokoṭikaṁ pañhaṁ puṭṭho neva sakkhati uggilituṁ, neva sakkhati ogilitun”ti.
sn42.9:3.8.eng: When you put this dilemma to him, the Buddha won’t be able to either spit it out or swallow it down.”

sn42.9:3.9.pli: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho asibandhakaputto gāmaṇi nigaṇṭhassa nāṭaputtassa paṭissutvā uṭṭhāyāsanā nigaṇṭhaṁ nāṭaputtaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho asibandhakaputto gāmaṇi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn42.9:3.9.eng: “Yes, sir,” replied Asibandhaka’s son. He got up from his seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Jain Ñātika, keeping him on his right. Then he went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:

sn42.9:4.1.pli: “Nanu, bhante, bhagavā anekapariyāyena kulānaṁ anuddayaṁ vaṇṇeti, anurakkhaṁ vaṇṇeti, anukampaṁ vaṇṇetī”ti?
sn42.9:4.1.eng: “Sir, don’t you in many ways praise kindness, protection, and sympathy for families?”

sn42.9:4.2.pli: “Evaṁ, gāmaṇi, tathāgato anekapariyāyena kulānaṁ anuddayaṁ vaṇṇeti, anurakkhaṁ vaṇṇeti, anukampaṁ vaṇṇetī”ti.

sn42.9:4.3.pli: “Atha kiñcarahi, bhante, bhagavā dubbhikkhe dvīhitike setaṭṭhike salākāvutte mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ cārikaṁ carati?
sn42.9:4.3.eng: “So what then are you doing, wandering together with this large Saṅgha of mendicants during a time of famine and scarcity, with blighted crops turned to straw?

sn42.9:4.4.pli: Ucchedāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno, anayāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno, upaghātāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno”ti.
sn42.9:4.4.eng: The Buddha is practicing to annihilate, collapse, and ruin families!”

sn42.9:4.5.pli: “Ito so, gāmaṇi, ekanavutikappe yamahaṁ anussarāmi, nābhijānāmi kiñci kulaṁ pakkabhikkhānuppadānamattena upahatapubbaṁ.
sn42.9:4.5.eng: “Well, chief, I recollect ninety-one eons back but I’m not aware of any family that’s been ruined merely by offering some cooked almsfood.

sn42.9:4.6.pli: Atha kho yāni tāni kulāni aḍḍhāni mahaddhanāni mahābhogāni pahūtajātarūparajatāni pahūtavittūpakaraṇāni pahūtadhanadhaññāni, sabbāni tāni dānasambhūtāni ceva saccasambhūtāni ca sāmaññasambhūtāni ca.
sn42.9:4.6.eng: Rather, rich, affluent, and wealthy families—with lots of gold and silver, lots of property and assets, and lots of money and grain—all acquired their wealth because of generosity, truth, and restraint.

sn42.9:4.7.pli: Aṭṭha kho, gāmaṇi, hetū, aṭṭha paccayā kulānaṁ upaghātāya.
sn42.9:4.7.eng: Chief, there are eight causes and reasons for the ruin of families.

sn42.9:4.8.pli: Rājato vā kulāni upaghātaṁ gacchanti, corato vā kulāni upaghātaṁ gacchanti, aggito vā kulāni upaghātaṁ gacchanti, udakato vā kulāni upaghātaṁ gacchanti, nihitaṁ vā ṭhānā vigacchati, duppayuttā vā kammantā vipajjanti, kule vā kulaṅgāroti uppajjati, yo te bhoge vikirati vidhamati viddhaṁseti, aniccatāyeva aṭṭhamīti.
sn42.9:4.8.eng: Their ruin stems from rulers, bandits, fire, or flood. Or they lose their savings. Or their business fails due to not applying themselves to work. Or a wastrel is born into the family who squanders and fritters away their wealth. And impermanence is the eighth.

sn42.9:4.9.pli: Ime kho, gāmaṇi, aṭṭha hetū, aṭṭha paccayā kulānaṁ upaghātāya.
sn42.9:4.9.eng: These are the eight causes and reasons for the ruin of families.

sn42.9:4.10.pli: Imesu kho, gāmaṇi, aṭṭhasu hetūsu, aṭṭhasu paccayesu saṁvijjamānesu yo maṁ evaṁ vadeyya:
sn42.9:4.10.eng: Given that these eight reasons are found, suppose someone says this:

sn42.9:4.11.pli: ‘ucchedāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno, anayāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno, upaghātāya bhagavā kulānaṁ paṭipanno’ti,
sn42.9:4.11.eng: ‘The Buddha is practicing to annihilate, collapse, and ruin families!’

sn42.9:4.12.pli: taṁ, gāmaṇi, vācaṁ appahāya taṁ cittaṁ appahāya taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissajjitvā yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ niraye”ti.
sn42.9:4.12.eng: Unless they give up that speech and that thought, and let go of that view, they will be cast down to hell.”

sn42.9:5.1.pli: Evaṁ vutte, asibandhakaputto gāmaṇi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn42.9:5.1.eng: When he said this, Asibandhaka’s son the chief said to the Buddha,

sn42.9:5.3.pli: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhagavā dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.
sn42.9:5.3.eng: From this day forth, may the Buddha remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

sn43.1:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, I will teach you the unconditioned and the path that leads to the unconditioned.

sn43.1:1.5.eng: The ending of greed, hate, and delusion.

sn43.1:1.7.eng: And what is the path that leads to the unconditioned?

sn43.1:1.9.eng: This is called the path that leads to the unconditioned.

sn43.1:2.1.pli: Iti kho, bhikkhave, desitaṁ vo mayā asaṅkhataṁ, desito asaṅkhatagāmimaggo.
sn43.1:2.1.eng: So, mendicants, I’ve taught you the unconditioned and the path that leads to the unconditioned.

sn43.1:2.2.pli: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, satthārā karaṇīyaṁ sāvakānaṁ hitesinā anukampakena anukampaṁ upādāya, kataṁ vo taṁ mayā.
sn43.1:2.2.eng: Out of sympathy, I’ve done what a teacher should do who wants what’s best for their disciples.

sn43.1:2.3.pli: Etāni, bhikkhave, rukkhamūlāni, etāni suññāgārāni. Jhāyatha, bhikkhave, mā pamādattha; mā pacchā vippaṭisārino ahuvattha. Ayaṁ vo amhākaṁ anusāsanī”ti.
sn43.1:2.3.eng: Here are these roots of trees, and here are these empty huts. Practice absorption, mendicants! Don’t be negligent! Don’t regret it later! This is my instruction to you.”

sn43.10:1.1.eng: “And what is the path that leads to the unconditioned?

sn43.10:1.2.pli: Satta bojjhaṅgā. Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, asaṅkhatagāmimaggo …pe….

sn43.11:1.1.eng: “And what is the path that leads to the unconditioned?

sn43.11:1.3.eng: This is called the path that leads to the unconditioned.

sn43.11:1.4.pli: Iti kho, bhikkhave, desitaṁ vo mayā asaṅkhataṁ, desito asaṅkhatagāmimaggo.
sn43.11:1.4.eng: So, mendicants, I’ve taught you the unconditioned and the path that leads to the unconditioned.

sn43.11:1.5.pli: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, satthārā karaṇīyaṁ sāvakānaṁ hitesinā anukampakena anukampaṁ upādāya kataṁ vo taṁ mayā.
sn43.11:1.5.eng: Out of sympathy, I’ve done what a teacher should do who wants what’s best for their disciples.

sn43.11:1.6.pli: Etāni, bhikkhave, rukkhamūlāni, etāni suññāgārāni. Jhāyatha, bhikkhave, mā pamādattha; mā pacchā vippaṭisārino ahuvattha. Ayaṁ vo amhākaṁ anusāsanī”ti.
sn43.11:1.6.eng: Here are these roots of trees, and here are these empty huts. Practice absorption, mendicants! Don’t be negligent! Don’t regret it later! This is my instruction to you.”
